questions.questionText: Species acting as both Bronsted acid and base is :
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
$${\left( {HS{O_4}} \right)^ - }\,\,\,$$ can accept and donate a proton

$${\left( {HS{O_4}} \right)^ - } + {H^ + } \to {H_2}S{O_4}\,\,\,$$ (acting as base)

$${\left( {HS{O_4}} \right)^ - } - {H^ + } \to {H_2}S{O_4}^{2 - }.\,\,\,$$ (acting as acid)
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText: 1 M NaCL and 1 M HCL are present in an aqueous solution. The solution is
options: not a buffer solution with pH < 7
not a buffer solution with pH > 7
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
NOTE : A buffer is a solution of weak acid and its salt with strong base and vice versa.

$$HCL$$ is strong acid and $$NaCL$$ is its salt with strong base.

$$pH$$ is less than $$7$$ due to $$HCL$$.
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText: When rain is accompanied by a thunderstorm, the collected rain water will have a pH value :
options: slightly higher than that when the thunderstorm is not there
which depends on the amount of dust in air
slightly lower than that of rain water without thunderstorm
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution: The rain water after thunderstorm contains dissolved acid and therefore the $$pH$$ is less than rain water without thunderstorm.
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is not true?
options: pH + pOH = 14 for all aqueous solutions
The pH of 1 $$\times$$ 10<sup>-8</sup> M HCl is 8
96,500 coulombs of electricity when passed through a CuSO<sub>4</sub> solution deposits 1 gram equivalent of copper at the cathode
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
$$pH$$ of an acidic solution should be less than $$7.$$ The reason is that from $${H_2}O.\left[ {{H^ + }} \right] = {10^{ - 7}}M$$ which cannot be neglected in comparison to $${10^{ - 8}}M.$$ The $$pH$$ can be calculated as.

from acid, $$\,\,\,\left[ {{H^ + }} \right] = {10^{ - 8}}M.$$

from $${H_2}O,\,\,\left[ {{H^ + }} \right] = {10^{ - 7}}M$$

$$\therefore$$ $$\,\,\,$$ Total $$\left[ {{H^ + }} \right] = {10^{ - 8}} + {10^{ - 7}}$$

$$ = {10^{ - 8}}\left( {1 + 10} \right) = 11 \times {10^{ - 8}}$$

$$\therefore$$ $$\,\,\,pH = - \log \left[ {{H^ + }} \right]$$

$$ = - \log 11 \times {10^{ - 8}}$$

$$ = - \left[ {\log 11 + 8\log \,10} \right]$$

$$ = - \left[ {1.0414 - 8} \right] = 6.9586$$
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText: What is the conjugate base of OH<sup>-</sup>?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
Conjugate acid-base pair differ by only one proton.

$$O{H^ - } \to {H^ + } + {O^2}^ - \,\,$$ Conjugate base of $$O{H^ - }$$ is $${O^{2 - }}$$
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText:
In a sautrated solution of the sparingly soluble strong electrolyte AgIO3 (Molecular mass = 283) the equilibrium which sets in is
AgIO3(s) $$\leftrightharpoons$$ Ag+(aq) + $$IO_3^-$$
If the solubility product constant Ksp of AgIO3 at a given temperature is 1.0 $$\times$$10−8, what is the mass of AgIO3 contained in 100 ml of its saturated solution?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
Let $$\,\,\,s = \,\,\,$$ solubility

$$Ag{\rm I}{O_3}\,\rightleftharpoons\,\mathop {A{g^ + }}\limits_s \,\,\mathop {{\rm I}{O_3}^ - }\limits_s $$

$${K_{sp}} = \left[ {A{g^ + }} \right]\left[ {{\rm I}{O_3}^ - } \right]$$

$$ = s \times s = {s^2}$$

Given $$\,\,\,{K_{sp}} = 1 \times {10^{ - 8}}$$

$$\therefore$$ $$\,\,\,s = \sqrt {{K_{sp}}} = \sqrt {1 \times {{10}^{ - 8}}} $$

$$ = 1.0 \times {10^4}\,\,mol/lit$$

$$ = 1.0 \times {10^{ - 4}} \times 283\,g/lit$$

(as Molecular mass of $$Ag\,{\rm I}{O_3} = 283$$ )

$$ = {{1.0 \times {{10}^{ - 4}} \times 283 \times 100} \over {1000}}gm/100ml$$

$$ = 2.83 \times {10^{ - 3}}\,gm/100\,ml$$

questions.questionText:
The pK<sub>a</sub> of a weak acid (HA) is 4.5. The pOH of an aqueous buffered solution of HA in which 50% of
the acid is ionized is :
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
For acidic buffer $$pH = p{K_a} + \log \left[ {{{salt} \over {acid}}} \right]$$

or $$\,\,\,pH = p{K_a} + \log {{\left[ {{A^ - }} \right]} \over {\left[ {HA} \right]}}$$

Given $$p{K_a} = 4.5\,\,$$ and acid is $$50\% $$ ionised.

$$\left[ {HA} \right] = \left[ {{A^ - }} \right]\,\,\,$$ (when acid is $$50\% $$ ionised)

$$\therefore$$ $$\,\,\,pH = p{K_a} + \log \,1$$

$$\therefore$$ $$\,\,\,pH = p{K_a} = 4.5$$

$$pOH = 14 - pH$$

$$ = 14 - 4.5$$

$$ = 9.5$$
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.

questions.questionText:
Four species are listed below
i. $$HCO_3^−$$
ii. $$H_3O^+$$
iii. $$HSO_4^−$$
iv. $$HSO_3F$$
Which one of the following is the correct sequence of their acid strength?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
The correct order of acidic strength of the given species in

$$\mathop {HS{O_3}F}\limits_{\left( {iv} \right)} > \mathop {{H_3}{O^ + }}\limits_{\left( {ii} \right)} > \mathop {HS{O_4}^ - }\limits_{\left( {iii} \right)} > \mathop {HC{O_3}^ - }\limits_{\left( i \right)} $$

or$$\,\,\,\left( i \right) < \left( {iii} \right) < \left( {ii} \right) < \left( {iv} \right)$$

It corresponds to choice $$(c)$$ which is correct answer.
questions.instruction: Choose the correct option out of the four choices.
